title: fagraea umbelliflora
intro: Fagraea umbelliflora is a plant species within the family Gentianaceae, commonly known as the frangipani flower or the "temple tree". Native to Southeast Asia, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Fagraea umbelliflora include its potential to treat fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve respiratory issues. Fagraea umbelliflora can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
